name: ai-compliance-mcp-server
description: 9-phase AI compliance audit pipeline — risk classification, bias assessment, adversarial testing, and W3C Verifiable Credential 2.0 certificate issuance

register_audit_context.description: Phase 1: Register an AI system for compliance auditing — captures system metadata, data lineage, and jurisdictional scope

scope_regulatory_baseline.description: Phase 2: Determine applicable regulatory frameworks (EU AI Act, GDPR, DPDP Act) based on jurisdictional scope and system type

classify_ai_risk.description: Phase 3: Classify AI system risk tier per EU AI Act Art. 5-6 — UNACCEPTABLE, HIGH, LIMITED, or MINIMAL with conformity path

assess_privacy_compliance.description: Phase 4: Perform GDPR Art. 35 DPIA, Art. 25 Privacy by Design checklist, and DPDP Act cross-border transfer analysis

run_bias_assessment.description: Phase 5: Multi-dimensional bias scan using Fairlearn and AIF360 — demographic parity, equalized odds, disparate impact

run_security_tests.description: Phase 6: Adversarial robustness testing via ART, input validation, and data minimisation checks per GDPR Art. 5(1)(c)

generate_explainability_report.description: Phase 7: Generate SHAP/LIME feature importance explanations and human-readable compliance report

issue_compliance_certificate.description: Phase 8: Issue W3C Verifiable Credential 2.0 with EdDSA signing, RFC 3161 timestamp, and Merkle proof anchoring

monitor_model_drift.description: Phase 9: Continuous drift detection and reaudit triggering for post-deployment monitoring compliance

audit://results/{run_id}.description: Audit results for a given run — all phase statuses, findings, and the issued certificate

audit://findings/{run_id}.description: All findings across phases for a given audit run, filterable by severity

audit://certificate/{run_id}.description: Issued W3C VC 2.0 certificate for a completed audit run
